sutta: is quite an important suta once the exalted 1 was on an arms round among the corsalis with a great following of mounts and on reaching the high road the exalted 1 saw in a certain spot a great fiery mess burning blazing flaming at the sight he stepped down off the road and sat down at the foot of a tree on the sea which was ready so seated he addressed amongst us see you amongst that great burning blazing flaming fiery mess yes lord what thank you monks which of the 2 were better that 1 should sit or lie beside that great burning blazing flaming firing mess caressing it or that 1 should sit or lie beside some maid whose hands and feet are soft and fair of noble birth or brahmin birth or your man birth caressing her and 1 of the monks said surely lord it were better to sit or lie and caress some made of noble brahmin or yomen birth whose hands and feet are soft and fair ill indeed lord worried to sit a lie and caress that great burning blazing flaming fiery mass monks i declare unto you i protest unto you it were better for a wicked man of evil nature unclean and of suspicious conduct full of secret actions no recluse though vowed there to unchase no vow to chastity rotten to the call lustful and vile to sit or lie and caress that great burning blazing flaming fiery mass and why on account of these monks he may suffer death or ill amounting to death but not for that reason on the breaking up of the body after death would he arise in the untoward way the ill way the abyss hell but when a wicked man of evil nature unclean and of suspicious nature etc lustful and vile sits or lies caressing some made of noble brahmin or yeoman birth whose feet and hands are soft and fair he does so to his harm and ill for many a day for on the breaking up of the body after death he is reborn in the unto 1 way the ill way the abyss in hell what thank you monks which of the 2 are better that a strong man with stout horse hair rope should bind and crush both 1's legs so that the rope cut the skin then the under skin then the flesh then the tendons and the bones and stay touching the marrow or that 1 should enjoy the salutations of noble of wealthy nobles wealthy brahmins wealthy your men surely lord it were better to enjoy the salutations of the wealthy he'll indeed worried lord that a strong man should bind and crush 1's legs to the marrow monks i declare unto you i protest unto you it was better for a wicked man of evil nature unclean and of suspicious conduct full of secret actions no recluse drove out there to unchase drove out to chastity rotten to the core lustful and vile that a strong man should bind and crush both his legs with rope so that it cut the skin the under skin the flesh the tendons the bones and stay touching the marrow and why on account of this monks he may suffer death or ill amounting to death but not for that reason on the breaking up of the body would he be reborn in hell but when a wicked man enjoys the salutations of wealthy nobles wealthy brahmins wealthy young men he does so to his harm and ill for many a day for on the breaking up of the body after death he is reborn in hell what in you monks which of the 2 were better that a strong man which sharpened salt clean in oil should smite 1 on the rum or that 1 should enjoy the obeisance of the wealthy surely lorne it were better to enjoy the obeisance of the wealthy ill indeed worried to be smitten on the rum monks i declare unto you i protest unto you it works better for a wicked man of evil nature unclean and of suspicious conduct etc that a strong man should with sharpened sword cleaned in oil smite him on the rum and why on account of this he may suffer death or ill amounting to death but not for that reason would he be reborn in hell but when a wicked man enjoys the obeisance of the wealthy he does so to his harm and ill for many a day for after death he is reborn in hell what thank you monks which of the 2 are better that a strong man should wrap 1's body around with red hot iron plates luminous glory glowing and fiery all that 1 should enjoy the rope a gift of faith of the wealthy surely lord it were better to enjoy the robe a gift of faith of the wealthy ill indeed worried to be wrapped in red hot iron plates monks i declare unto you i protest unto you it were better for a wicked man of evil nature unclean etc that a strong man should wrap his body around with red hot iron plates luminous glowing and fiery and why on account of this he may suffer death or ill amounting to death but not for that reason would he be reborn in hell but when a wicked man enjoys the robe a gift of faith of the wealthy he does so to his harm and ill for many a day for after death he is reborn in hell but thank you monks which of the 2 were better that a strong man with the red hot iron spike luminous glowing and fiery should open 1's mouth and should thrust there in a red hot copper ball luminous glowing and fiery so that it burns the lips burn the tongue burn the throat burn the belly and take along with it the intestines and the bowels and pass out through the anus all that 1 should enjoy the arms a gift of faith of the wealthy surely lord it were better to enjoy the arms a gift of faith of the wealthy he'll indeed worried to have a red hot copper ball thrust into 1's mouth monks i declare unto you i protest unto you it was better for a wicked man of evil nature unclean etc that a strong man should open his mouth with a red hot spike and should thrust therein a red hot copper ball which would burn his lips burn the tongue burn the throat burn the belly and take along within the intestines and the bowels and pass out through the anus and why on account of this he may suffer death or ill amounting to death but not for that reason would he be reborn in hell but when a wicked man enjoys the arms a gift of faith of the wealthy he does so to his harm and ill for many a day for after death he is reborn in hell but thank you monks which of the 2 are better that the strong man should cease 1 by the head or by the shoulders and should force 1 to sit or lie on a red hot iron couch or red hot iron bed luminous glowing and fiery all that 1 should enjoy the couch orbit the gift of faith of the surely lord it was better to enjoy the couch or bed a gift of the wealthy a gift of faith of the wealthy you'll indeed worry to be forced to sit or lie on a red hot iron couch or bed monks i declare unto you i protest unto you it were better for a wicked man of evil nature unclean etc that a strong man should cease him by the head or by the shoulders and should force him to sit or lie on a red-hot iron couch or bed and why on account of this he may suffer death or ill amounting to death but not for that reason would he be reborn in hell but when a wicked man enjoys a couch orbit a gift of faith of the wealthy he does so to his harm and ill for many a day for after death he is reborn in hell but thank you monks which of the 2 were better that the strong man should seize 1 feet upmost and head down and shoot her 1 into a red hot copper cauldron luminous glowing and fiery and there being boiled to be whirled now up now down now cross ways like bubbling scum or that 1 should enjoy the lodging a gift of faith of wealthy nobles wealthy brahmins or wealthy your men surely lord it were better to enjoy the lodging a gift of faith of wealthy nobles wealthy brahmins or wealthy young men ill indeed lord that a strong man should cease 1 feet upmost and head down and should hurl 1 into a red hot copper cauldron luminous glowing and fiery where being boiled 1 would be wall now up now down now cross ways like bubbling scum monks i declare unto you i protest unto you it was better for a wicked man of evil nature unclean and of suspicious conduct full of secret actions no recluse though vowed there to unchase no vow to chastity rotten to the core lustful and vile to be seized feet utmost and hid down by a strong man and hurled into a red hot copper cauldron luminous glowing and fiery where being boiled he would be whirled now up now down now cross ways like bubbling scum and why on account of this he may suffer death or ill amounting to death but not for that reason on the breaking up of the body after death would he arise in the untoward way the ill way the abyss hell but when a wicked man of evil nature unclean and of suspicious conduct lustful and vile enjoys the lodging a gift of faith of wealthy nobles wealthy brahmanas or wealthy young men he does so to his harm and evil for many a day for on the breaking up of the body after death he is reborn in hell wherefore mounts train yourselves thus wise of whomsoever we enjoy the requisites that is to say the road arms lodging and medicines unto them such services will become very fruitful very profitable and unto us also this going forth will not become a barren thing but fruitful with issue train yourselves in this way amongst monks for 1 who can see his own wheel to strive earnestly is well worthwhile for 1 who can see another's wheel to strive earnestly is well worthwhile for 1 who can see the wheel both of self and others to strive earnestly is well worthwhile thus spoke the exalted 1 now while this exposition was being delivered from the mouth of as many as 16 months hot blood gushed forth 60 more gave up the training and returned to the lower life saying heart is the task of the exalted 1 very harm is the task of the exalted 1 but the hearts of 60 others became without attachment and free from the asapas that's the end of the sutta
commentary: this is quite an interesting and quite a strong suta the here the buddha is giving a warning that a fraud or evil monk will end up in hell and um the descriptions of the different kinds of suffering that the buddha described now actually um in some other sutas and we can see these are the types of suffering that a person undergoes you know when a person falls into hell and this kind of suffering in hell is for a long time and that's why the buddha said now it is better to suffer these various kinds of suffering now than to be a fraud a rotten monk and accept all the offerings of healthy devotees because after that that person if he were to be that kind of a evil monk then he would have to suffer all these types of suffering many many times and for a long time in hell so it is better for a monk to disrupt if he cannot live the holy life purely that is why like in thailand it seems there's a saying that there are many people with yellow ropes in hell and also you find towards the end many of the monks couldn't accept this teaching by the buddha and some of them came forth from the mouth and some disrobed and returned to the lower life but those who realized the teaching they were freed of the ask of us and became arahants sometimes some uh some monks who are not real monks and they don't like sutas like this because it is very how do you see it it's is right on on spot on as they say and this is the words users are quite strong with them now we come to the nexuta 7
